data.title: Suffolk Taps Industry Veteran Louis Martinelli to Scale Its Freedom Source Supply Chain Vertical and Reinforce Its Seamless Platform Delivery
data.description: Headquartered in Boston, Suffolk, a leading construction company, has appointed Louis Martinelli as the Head of Freedom Source. This direct-to-manufacturer procurement engine aims to enhance cost transparency and competitive pricing, boost supply reliability, and streamline the design-to-construction workflow for clients. Louis Martinelli will report to Pat Lucey, Suffolk Northeast and National Centers of Excellence President. This move is part of Suffolk's effort to innovate and improve its services.

data.title: Distribution Solutions Group Announces 2025 Full Year and Fourth Quarter Results
data.description: Fort Worth, Texas - Distribution Solutions Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: DSGR), a leading specialty distribution company, revealed its consolidated results for the fourth quarter ending on December 31, 2025. The report can be accessed alongside an earnings presentation on their website at https://investor.distributionsolutionsgroup.com/news/events. The disclosed data includes selected unaudited operational outcomes. For further details on the reconciliations of Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) to non-GAAP measures, refer to the provided resources.

data.title: LabVantage Solutions Introduces LabVantage CORTEX, Advancing Its LIMS Platform for AI-Driven Laboratory Operations
data.description: A significant development in Somerset, New Jersey reveals that 75% of laboratories are integrating artificial intelligence (AI) technology into their operations. One such innovative solution is LabVantage CORTEX, which offers a tailored AI ecosystem aimed at enhancing efficiency, precision, and data analysis capabilities for customers. This advancement signifies a prominent shift towards AI adoption within the lab industry, highlighting the growing importance of incorporating cutting-edge technologies for improved performance and outcomes.

data.title: AI Momentum, Material Margin Expansion, and Cash Flow Growth Highlight Wiley’s Third Quarter 2026
data.description: In Hoboken, New Jersey, global leader Wiley (NYSE: WLY) released its third-quarter results for the period ending January 31, 2026. The GAAP performance compared to the previous year showed a 1% increase in revenue, totaling $410 million. Operating income saw a significant boost, rising by 21% to $63 million. Diluted Earnings Per Share (EPS) also demonstrated a notable improvement, reaching $0.56 compared to a loss of ($0.43) in the previous year. Adjusted results were maintained at a constant rate. Wiley continues to be at the forefront of providing authoritative content and research intelligence to drive scientific discovery, innovation, and learning.

data.title: John Wiley & Sons: Q3 Earnings Insights
data.description: The latest quarterly earnings report from John Wiley & Sons (NYSE: WLY) was released on March 5, 2026, at 07:30 AM. The company surpassed estimated earnings by 12.79%, posting an EPS of $0.97 compared to the estimated $0.86. Revenue also showed growth, increasing by $5.41 million from the previous year. This positive performance follows a trend of consistently exceeding EPS expectations. For further details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Insights into Ranpak Hldgs Q4 Earnings
data.description: Ranpak Hldgs (NYSE: PACK) released its Q4 earnings report on Thursday, March 5, 2026 at 07:30 AM. The earnings showed that Ranpak Hldgs fell short of the estimated earnings by -450.0%, reporting an EPS of $-0.11 compared to an estimate of $-0.02. The revenue saw an increase of $6.90 million from the previous year's same period. Looking at their previous earnings, the company also missed on EPS. For more details, visit Benzinga.com for the full story.
